title: Phemeranthus thompsonii
intro: Phemeranthus thompsonii, also known as desert rockjasmine, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Phemeranthus thompsonii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Desert rockjasmine is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often employed in traditional medicine to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory symptoms.

It is classified as a succulent plant with a rosette-shaped growth habit.
